title: Today's Tech Brief: The Best Robotic Pool Cleaners of 2026: Beatbot, iGarden, Dreame
summary: A look at the latest robotic pool cleaners and the intersection of AI and truth.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's tech brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you. First up, if you're a pool owner, you know how much fun it is to clean the thing. Said no one ever. But, there are some new robotic pool cleaners on the market that might make the task a little more bearable. According to Wired, the best robotic pool cleaners of 2026 are from Beatbot, iGarden, and Dreame. I mean, who wouldn't want to spend their summer days lounging by the pool, rather than scrubbing it clean?

These robotic pool cleaners are designed to make quick work of dirt, debris, and other gunk that accumulates in your pool. I'm talking about a significant upgrade from the old leaf-skimming-net-and-hose combo. My read is that these devices are a great example of how technology can make our lives a little easier, and a lot less frustrating. No more tedious cleaning, just more time for the fun stuff. Plus, I'm guessing they'll be a hit with pool owners who are tired of dealing with pesky pool-maintenance tasks on their days off.

The Best Robotic Pool Cleaners of 2026: Beatbot, iGarden, Dreame is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Swimming pools are fun. Cleaning swimming pools is not fun. I learned this simple logic as a kid growing up in and around pools—it’s the only way to survive summer in Houston. Four years ago, I became a pool owner myself, and I found that the rule still holds. Jumping into the pool on a hot day remains a rare treat, but if the pool is filled with leaves and... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.
